channel.title: golang on Abhishek's blog
channel.description: Recent content in golang on Abhishek's blog

item.title: Write your Kubernetes Infrastructure as Go code - Combine cdk8s with AWS CDK
item.description: In an earlier blog post you saw how to use cdk8s with AWS Controllers for Kubernetes (also known as ACK), thanks to the fact that you can import existing Kubernetes Custom Resource Definitions using cdk8s!

item.title: Manage Redis on AWS from Kubernetes
item.description: Using AWS Controller for Kubernetes and CDK for Kubernetes In this blog post, you will learn how to use ACK with Amazon EKS for creating a Redis cluster on AWS (with Amazon MemoryDB).

item.title: Use AWS App Runner, DynamoDB and CDK to deploy and run a Cloud native Go app
item.description: Earlier, I wrote about a Serverless URL shortener application on AWS using DynamoDB, AWS Lambda and API Gateway. In this blog post, we will deploy that as a REST API on AWS App Runner and continue to use DynamoDB as the database.

item.title: Write your Kubernetes Infrastructure as Go code - Extend cdk8s with custom Constructs
item.description: Build a Wordpress deployment as a cdk8s construct Constructs are the fundamental building block of cdk8s (Cloud Development Kit for Kubernetes) - an open-source framework (part of CNCF) with which you can define your Kubernetes applications using regular programming languages (instead of yaml).

item.title: Build a Serverless URL shortener with Go
item.description: Using AWS Lambda, DynamoDB and API Gateway This blog post covers how to build a Serverless URL shortener application using Go.

item.title: Write your Kubernetes Infrastructure as Go code - Using Custom Resource Definitions with cdk8s
item.description: cdk8s (Cloud Development Kit for Kubernetes) is an an open-source framework (part of CNCF) using which you can define your Kubernetes applications with regular programming languages (instead of yaml).

item.title: Learn how to use DynamoDB Streams with AWS Lambda and Go
item.description: Replicate DynamoDB data from one table to another This blog post will help you get quickly started with DynamoDB Streams and AWS Lambda using Go.

item.title: Write your Kubernetes Infrastructure as Go code - cdk8s-plus in action!
item.description: The previous blog post covered how to get started with cdk8s (Cloud Development Kit for Kubernetes), that is an an open-source framework (part of CNCF) using which you can define your Kubernetes applications using regular programming languages (instead of yaml).

item.title: Write your Kubernetes Infrastructure as Go code - Getting started with cdk8s
item.description: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) is a well established paradigm and refers to the standard practice of treating infrastructure (network, disk, storage, databases, message queues etc.

item.title: Build a Twitter Leaderboard app with Redis and AWS Lambda (part 2)
item.description: This is the second blog post of this two-part series that uses a practical application to demonstrate how to integrate Redis with AWS Lambda.

item.title: Build Cloud-Native apps with AWS App Runner, Redis and AWS CDK
item.description: Run your Go app on AWS App Runner service, integrate it with MemoryDB for Redis and use AWS CDK to package and deploy the app along with infrastructure

item.title: Build a Twitter Leaderboard app with Redis and AWS Lambda (part 1)
item.description: Hello and welcome 👋🏼 to this two-part blog series that uses a practical application to demonstrate how to integrate Redis with AWS Lambda.

item.title: Getting started with Redis on AWS - the easy way!
item.description: This quick-start uses AWS Cloud9 IDE to help you get up and running with MemoryDB for Redis, quickly When I was initially exploring some of the AWS services (such as MemoryDB, MSK, Elasticache for Redis etc.

item.title: Use Lambda Function URL to write a Serverless app backed by DynamoDB
item.description: This is a Go Lambda function that’s packaged and deployed using AWS SAM Lambda Function URL is a relatively new feature (at the time of writing this blog) that provides dedicated HTTP(S) endpoint for your Lambda function.

item.title: Package and deploy a Lambda function as a Docker container with AWS CDK
item.description: Deploy a Serverless backend for Slack using Infrastructure-as-code (IaaC) One of my previous blog post covered how to build a Serverless backend for Slack using by using Lambda Function URL as a webhook.

item.title: Using AWS Lambda Function URL to build a Serverless backend for Slack
item.description: A combination of AWS Lambda and Amazon API Gateway is a widely-used architecture for serverless microservices and API based solutions. They enable developers to focus on their applications, instead of spending time provisioning and managing servers.

item.title: How to use Azure Go SDK to manage Azure Data Explorer clusters
item.description: Getting started with Azure Data Explorer using the Go SDK covered how to use the Azure Data Explorer Go SDK to ingest and query data from azure data explorer to ingest and query data.

item.title: Tutorial: Getting started with Azure Data Explorer using the Go SDK
item.description: With the help of an example, this blog post will walk you through how to use the Azure Data explorer Go SDK to ingest data from a Azure Blob storage container and query it programmatically using the SDK.

item.title: Write your Kubernetes Infrastructure as Go code - Manage AWS services
item.description: Deploy DynamoDB and a client app using cdk8s along with AWS Controller for Kubernetes AWS Controllers for Kubernetes (also known as ACK) are built around the Kubernetes extension concepts of Custom Resource and Custom Resource Definitions.
